text: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) for task-specific performance is a ubiquitous practice across the industry, and the speed and ease of doing so is vital for successful model deployment. Achieving this efficiency while supporting a wide variety of models is typically a substantial engineering task. We present LLaMA-Factory, a flexible framework for instantiating recent fine-tuning acceleration techniques while being highly scalable and easy to use. Via the accompanying no-code web interface LLaMA-Board, users can fine-tune to optimal performance any of the 100+ different LLMs we currently support. We measure the trade-offs of LLaMA-Factory between high-performance model behavior, overall training time, and overall memory consumption, finding that it is competitive to and often better than other accelerations approaches. Finally, we share our findings and open-source release of this project which has so far garnered more than 25,000 GitHub stars and 3,000 forks (https://github.com/hiyouga/LLaMA-Factory).
explanation: The text has a polished, promotional-technical tone with project-specific names and a real-looking GitHub link, which can be produced by both humans and AI. The concrete-sounding metrics (100+ models, 25,000 stars, 3,000 forks) are plausible but not diagnostic, so the passage could plausibly be human-authored or AI-generated.
